element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Flight control via wing-flapping rhythm; ascent/hover/descent via button press frequency
Spatial navigation anchors; safe zones above lava
Introduction to collision-based combat; jousting mechanic (higher lance wins)
Risk/reward mechanic; must collect for points or egg hatches into stronger knight
Indestructible Lava Troll hazard; instant life loss if touched
More aggressive AI; higher point value than Bounders
Highest difficulty enemy type; highest point value
Optional high-risk/high-reward target; hunting behavior adds dynamic threat

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating all enemies in current wave
Collecting fallen enemy eggs for points
Wave completion transitioning to next level
typical_tension_triggers: Uncollected egg hatching into new enemy
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating wave-based intensity; each wave introduces more enemies and upgraded enemy types (Bounder → Hunter → Shadow Lord); pterodactyl interrupts calm collection periods; constant vertical resource management (altitude vs. lava threat)

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Pterodactyl appearance (bonus points opportunity); egg collection vs. hatching (risk/reward positioning)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Single enemy type (Bounder) introduced; player learns flight control and basic jousting with low-pressure encounters
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Wave progression introduces Hunter enemies; increased enemy count per wave; pterodactyl mechanic adds time pressure
difficulty_curve.late_game: Shadow Lord enemies (highest difficulty type); wave complexity escalates; cumulative pressure from mixed enemy types; egg management becomes critical resource challenge
notable_spikes: Introduction of Hunter-type enemies (behavioral shift from Bounder)
Introduction of Shadow Lord enemies (final difficulty tier)
Each wave completion: enemy count and type composition increases
Pterodactyl appearance: interrupts egg-collection windows, adds aggressive hunting element

notable_level_patterns.description: Game structured as repeating 'waves' of enemy groups. Each completed wave triggers next wave with increased difficulty (more enemies, upgraded enemy types). No traditional level structure; progression through escalating enemy composition.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Wave 1 (Bounders) → Wave 2 (mixed Bounder/Hunter) → Wave N (Shadow Lords dominant)

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-screen arena with platforms at multiple altitudes and lava hazard at bottom. Player must manage altitude for combat positioning (higher lance wins jousts) vs. lava threat (Lava Troll). Platforms serve as safe refuge and enemy encounter zones.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Floating platform layout creates vertical risk/reward zones

notable_level_patterns.description: No projectiles; all combat via aerial collision with jousting mechanic (vertical position determines winner). Equal-height collisions create repulsion, teaching positioning skill. Defeated enemies drop collectible eggs.

Defeated enemies become eggs falling toward lava. Player faces binary choice: collect egg for points (positions player near lava risk) or leave uncollected (egg hatches into new, stronger knight). Risk/reward decision gate.
After fixed time in wave, pterodactyl spawns to hunt player. High-point bonus for defeat but aggressive behavior interrupts safe egg-collection periods. Acts as difficulty spike and timer mechanic.
